summary: Matched a set of gm_1B03 game-mode/minor-scene functions and one gmvsmelee helper by replacing opaque buffers with typed match data, correcting MatchExitInfo layout variants, adding repeated VsModeData fields in gm main state, and tightening prototypes/linkage. The decomp.dev bot reported 18 new matches and +1120 matched bytes, including gm_801B13B8, gm_801B2AF8, gm_801B0B8C, several OnInit thunks, and gm_801A5F00.
what_changed: Implemented previously placeholder gm_1B03 functions including gm_801B0B8C, gm_801B13B8, gm_801B14A0, gm_801B16A8, gm_801B2AF8, gm_801B2B7C_OnInit, gm_801B8D14, gm_801B8D60_OnInit, gm_801B8F70_OnInit, gm_801B9134, gm_801B9180_OnInit, gm_801B95B0_OnInit, gm_801BA0C4_OnInit, gm_801BA2C4_OnInit, gm_801BA4C4_OnInit, gm_801BA6BC_OnInit, and gm_801BA8B4_OnInit.

Changed large opaque globals in gm_1B03.c into typed objects such as static MatchExitInfo gm_80480F20, static struct MatchExitInfo2 gm_804831A8, and static StartMeleeData gm_8048E4C0, then updated MinorScene initializers to pass their addresses.
Added MinorScene tables gm_803DD888_MinorScenes and gm_803DD8B8_MinorScenes, along with related statics gm_804D68B0, gm_804D68B8, and gm_804D68BC.
Introduced struct MatchExitInfo2 in types.h with two leading ints before MatchEnd, distinct from MatchExitInfo which keeps three leading ints before MatchEnd.
Changed gm_80177724 in gm_1601.h and gmresultplayer.c to take struct MatchExitInfo2* and memzero that exact object size.
Adjusted gm_801A5F00 in gmvsmelee.c to use struct MatchExitInfo2* and copy gm_80479D98.match_end into exit_info->match_end, completing its match.
Expanded struct gmm_x0 to expose repeated VsModeData fields at offsets unk_6D0, unk_810, unk_950, unk_A90, unk_BD0, unk_D10, unk_E50, unk_F90, unk_10D0, unk_1210, and unk_1350.
Changed MatchEnd.frame_count from int to u32 and adjusted gm_801A5AF0 to avoid an explicit u32 cast and use !gm_80167140(...) style.
Made gm_80479D98 non-static in gmvsmelee.static.h so gm_1B03.c can extern it as MatchExitInfo.
Corrected config/GALE01/symbols.txt size for gm_804D68A4 from 0xC to 0x8 while keeping a separate gm_804D68A4_pad in source for an apparent file-boundary/padding issue.
Updated many gm_1B03.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void OnInit(void) or void function(MinorScene*) where implementations or call tables established exact signatures.
smart_moves: Separated MatchExitInfo and MatchExitInfo2 instead of forcing one layout, which made gm_80177724 and gm_801A5F00 type-correct and allowed memcpy/memzero-size-sensitive code to match.

Typed scene payload globals and then used &global in MinorScene initializer slots, aligning C expression shape with data-table pointer semantics rather than relying on array-to-pointer decay from UNK_T buffers.
Exposed the repeated VsModeData blocks inside gmMainLib_804D3EE0 state so numerous OnInit functions become simple, repeatable gm_80167B50(&gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_XXXX) thunks.
Preserved likely data ordering/file-boundary constraints by correcting gm_804D68A4 symbol size to 0x8 and retaining an explicit gm_804D68A4_pad source object.
Converted a shared gmvsmelee static into a file-visible MatchExitInfo symbol, enabling gm_1B03 matches that reference gm_80479D98.match_end directly.
Used small signature cleanups in headers to remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS only where evidence from implementations was available.
decomp_lessons: When a function only memzeros an argument, exact struct size still matters; if two call sites expect similar trailing data with different leading fields, create a second struct variant rather than guessing a universal layout.
MinorScene initializer matching can depend on whether the source uses a typed object address or an array name; replacing UNK_T arrays with typed globals can unlock both data and code matches.

Repeated one-line OnInit functions often indicate an array/sequence of same-sized substructures in a global state struct; naming each offset as a VsModeData field can collapse many unknown thunks into straightforward matches.
Symbol file object sizes should track the actual decompiled object, and padding/file-boundary artifacts can be represented explicitly rather than hiding them inside an oversized symbol.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching work: exact void(void), void(MinorScene*), and struct-specific pointer types reduce casts and produce more natural C for later functions.
Unsigned fields such as frame_count can eliminate casts in arithmetic expressions and improve gmvsmelee matching.
naming_conventions: Unidentified game-mode functions keep address-based names such as gm_801B13B8 and gm_801B2AF8.
Lifecycle functions use suffixes like _OnInit, _OnLoad, _OnLeave, and OnInit prototypes were normalized to void name(void).
MinorScene data arrays follow gm_803Dxxxx_MinorScenes naming.
Known-but-still-offset-based globals use gm_804xxxxx names, while typed struct fields in gmm_x0 use offset-derived names such as unk_950, unk_E50, and unk_1350.
The second match-exit layout was named struct MatchExitInfo2, indicating weak semantic knowledge but concrete layout distinction.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use typed globals plus address-of operators in static data tables to control relocation/data initializer output.
Split a near-identical struct layout into MatchExitInfo and MatchExitInfo2 to get correct offsets and object sizes for memzero and match_end copies.
Represent file-boundary padding explicitly with gm_804D68A4_pad after shrinking gm_804D68A4 to its real 0x8 size.
Turn repeated init stubs into calls to gm_80167B50 over specific VsModeData offsets in gmMainLib_804D3EE0.
Prefer natural unsigned arithmetic when field evidence supports it, as with MatchEnd.frame_count becoming u32 and removing an explicit cast.
For scene transition helpers, source shape matters: gm_801A5AF0 was cleaned to initialize match_exit_info at declaration and use a compact if/else condition.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 34.48%, +0.03%, +1120 bytes.
decomp.dev bot listed 18 new matches: 17 in main/melee/gm/gm_1B03 and gm_801A5F00 in main/melee/gm/gmvsmelee.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that replace MatchExitInfo2 with a semantic name or further refine MatchExitInfo layouts.
Find additional gm_1B03 OnInit thunks that call gm_80167B50 on gmMainLib_804D3EE0 VsModeData offsets.
Search for other symbol-size corrections involving explicit _pad globals around file boundaries in .sbss.
Investigate remaining gm_1B03 placeholders near gm_801B0FB8, gm_801B138C, gm_801B14DC, gm_801B16C8, and gm_801B2790.
Check whether the duplicate include of melee/gm/types.h in gm_1B03.c was left intentionally or cleaned up later.
